$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the PROVISION-ON-THE-FLY seam of v4's
CharactersRepository.update (Phase-2, the store-backed capstone).
Proves applyDocumentStoreWriteOverlay's no-vault-but-managed-field branch: when
a character has NO linked vault and the patch carries managed fields, v4 logs an
error, calls ensureCharacterVault(character) to provision a FRESH vault, re-reads
the FK it set, verifies it linked, then routes the patch's managed fields onto
the new vault.
FIXTURE: built by running v4's REAL repos.characters.create(character) once, THEN
wiping the vault (delete every mount-index store row + NULL the character's
characterDocumentMountPointId). The character is left vault-less, and no
same-name populated store survives, so the adopt search finds nothing and a fresh
vault is provisioned. Both sides operate on a COPY of this SAME baked+wiped
fixture; the character id is read back (SELECT id FROM characters).
OP: { identity, description, manifesto, personality, exampleDialogues, title,
firstMessage, talkativeness, pronouns, aliases } — a full managed-field
patch (no slim column), so the slim _update is skipped (updatedAt NOT
bumped) and the provisioned vault holds these values.
REINDEX: the oracle drives the REAL update (no QUILLTAP_JOB_CHILD), so
reindexSingleFile runs; the differential pins the link chunkCount and excludes
doc_mount_chunks.
NORMALIZATION: minted-values remap with ONE shared id-map across all six tables.
The provisioned mount point + every file/document/link/folder id is minted, so
every FK verifies by relationship; timestamps -> <ts>; chunkCount -> <cc>. The
managed-field content diffs EXACTLY.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key. Safe to commit.

patch.identity: A wandering scholar of forgotten tongues.
patch.exampleDialogues: Q: Who are you? A: A reader of what remains.
patch.title: the scholar
